text.title: Risk Management Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns observed in real deployments. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Risk register never reviewed after creation; 6-month-old register still cited.
antipattern.root-cause: A stale register is worse than none because it creates false confidence.
antipattern.fix: Mandatory weekly 15-min review; flag register as 'stale' after 30 days no update.

antipattern.symptom: More than 70% of risks scored Medium/Medium.
antipattern.root-cause: Probability ratings cluster around 'Medium' — too safe; LLMs avoid commitment.
antipattern.fix: Force normal distribution; require justification per rating ('M because vendor missed last 2 SLAs').

antipattern.symptom: 'Accept' response used without a contingency budget line.
antipattern.root-cause: Accept is the most-abused response: lets teams skip planning while pretending to manage.
antipattern.fix: Require contingency_amount or contingency_plan on every Accept entry; reject otherwise.

antipattern.symptom: Materialised risks tracked as both Risk and Issue.
antipattern.root-cause: Double-tracking inflates the risk count and confuses status.
antipattern.fix: On materialisation, move to Issues and mark register row 'closed: materialised'.
